page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance belonging to the ecs.ce4 family. It is provisioned via the KVM hypervisor and features 4 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.ce4.xlarge delivers modest performance, reflecting its older processor architecture. In CPU-intensive workloads, the instance registers weak single-core and multi-core scores in stress-ng and Passmark tests, placing in the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large-block read operations falling into the bottom 10% at 22.44 GB/sec. However, it maintains average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside moderate text processing capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.ce4.xlarge offers a high memory capacity relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance compute cycles. Due to its dedicated CPU allocation, it avoids the performance variability of shared-core instances. This server is best suited for legacy database hosting, moderate web serving, memory-intensive caching layers, and lightweight text or data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth metrics generally falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers, though it maintains average speeds in single-threaded compression and text processing. Given its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the ecs.ce4.xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high-performance compute. It is suitable for legacy database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose memory-bound applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for legacy databases and memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A dedicated memory-optimized virtual server offering high memory density for legacy databases and caching workloads without local storage.
